title: Trump admin allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI model to some companies, government agencies
description: Anthropic disabled access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models to comply with a government export control directive citing "national security authorities."

title: Aid Groups Flock to Venezuela In Search-and-Rescue Frenzy
description: Two of the US military ships used in a blockade meant to pressure Nicolás Maduro have headed back toward Venezuela, this time with rescue teams, equipment and medical aid after devastating twin earthquakes struck the country this week.

title: Philippine Government Plans to Raise Budget by 6% Next Year
description: The Philippines plans to increase the budget by 6% next year to 7.2 trillion pesos ($117 billion), according to the budget department.

title: Bolivia Moves to Flexible Exchange-Rate System After 15 Years
description: Bolivia is moving to a flexible exchange-rate system to strengthen macroeconomic stability, its Finance Ministry announced Friday.

title: Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools
description: Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state.

title: Just how much trouble is Canada's economy in?
description: Five charts help illustrate the state of Canada's economy - and how it's performing compared to other wealthy nations.

title: Trump threatens 100% tariff on European nations over tech tax
description: The US president says "Numerous European countries" have been discussing bringing in such a levy.

title: Exiled Chefs From Myanmar Give Burmese Food Chance to Shine on International Stage
description: Much of the world is unfamiliar with the cuisine, but an exodus from Myanmar after a coup has made it more visible on the international stage.

title: Nations Send Rescue Teams and Aid to Venezuela After Earthquakes
description: The number of the dead continues to rise as search-and-rescue teams descended upon the country to dig out people buried in rubble.

title: Convicted Rapist Who Fled to Scotland and Faked His Own Death Dies in Utah
description: Nicholas Rossi, 38, raped two girlfriends in 2008 and later fled to Scotland, prosecutors said. An attentive nurse treating him for Covid in 2021 identified him.

description: A snapshot of the toll of the country’s twin quakes.

description: Here’s how to contribute to organizations that are aiding the rescue and recovery efforts in Venezuela.

title: Rescue efforts turn to recovery as aftershocks shake Venezuela
description: Rescue workers in one Caracas neighbourhood say no help has arrived, two days after twin quakes tore through the city.

title: Spain beat Uruguay 1-0 to clinch World Cup Group H top spot
description: Spain wins World Cup group, beating Uruguay 1-0 as Fernando Muslera’s error sends two-time champions home.

description: The US has struck Iran in retaliation for what it says was an Iranian attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Iran war live: US strikes Iran after attack on vessel in Strait of Hormuz
description: Iran's IRGC says it targeted US military in region after Trump administration launched attack on the country.

title: CU in China: Why South Korean retail giant is adopting ‘online first’ strategy
description: South Korean convenience store giant CU has been testing the waters in China with a limited online trial operation, marking a different approach from other multinational brands that bet big on physical outlets. The brand, operated by BGF Retail which has around 18,600 stores in South Korea, is being cautious to start with. Together with its Chinese partner Ningshing Ubay, it is only selling 11 products on e-commerce platform Tmall under Alibaba Group, which also owns the South China Morning...

title: Growth agenda: Hong Kong vows stronger exchange with reforms, bond futures and gold push
description: Hong Kong is pressing ahead with an overhaul of listing rules and the launch of new product initiatives, the city’s deputy finance chief said on Friday as the bourse operator marked 26 years as a publicly traded company. Speaking at the anniversary ceremony of Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing (HKEX), Deputy Financial Secretary Michael Wong Wai-lun outlined reforms under review, including optimising weighted voting rights, easing secondary listings by overseas issuers, and expanding flexibility...

title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
description: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

title: Wall Street Week | USMCA: Can North America’s Trade Deal Survive?
description: This week, a special edition of Wall Street Week on the USMCA trade deal. The renegotiations are testing the future of an auto industry built on decades of cross-border integration. And, American farmers increasingly depend on exports to Canada and Mexico as other overseas markets become more difficult to access. Plus, years of infrastructure investment have created an energy market that crosses borders more easily than political narratives suggest. Later, can three countries with different priorities still agree on a common economic vision? (Source: Bloomberg)
